IXWireEnd, IXWriteableObject

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXObject
    Interface allowing property and attribute access to objects.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getAttribute(java.lang.String name)
      Get the value of the attribute given the name of the attribute.Attributes are listed at Attribute List
      java.util.Set<IXValue> getAttributes()
      Get the attributes owned by the object.Attributes are listed at Attribute List
      java.lang.String getBaseID()
      Get the Base ID of the object
      java.lang.String getID()
      Get the ID of the object
      java.lang.String getParentID()
      Get the Parent ID of the object
      java.util.Set<IXValue> getProperties()
      Get the properties owned by the object.
      java.lang.String getProperty(java.lang.String name)
      Get the value of the property given the name of the property.
      IXSymbol getSymbol()
      Get the referenced symbol object, if this is an object with symbol.
      java.lang.String toHTML()
      Converts this object into a HTML clickable reference (HREF) in the Output Window of some Capital applications: Capital Logic, Capital Design, Capital Integrator and Capital HarnessXC.
      java.lang.String toHTML(java.lang.String message)
      Converts this object into a HTML clickable reference (HREF) in the Output Window of some Capital applications: Capital Logic, Capital Design, Capital Integrator and Capital HarnessXC.

    • Method Detail

      • getProperties

        java.util.Set<IXValue> getProperties()
        Get the properties owned by the object.
        Returns:
        a Set of the properties of the object if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getAttributes

        java.util.Set<IXValue> getAttributes()
        Get the attributes owned by the object.Attributes are listed at Attribute List
        Returns:
        a Set of the attributes of the object if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getProperty

        java.lang.String getProperty(java.lang.String name)
        Get the value of the property given the name of the property.
        Parameters:
        name - name of the property value being sought.
        Returns:
        the property value if it exists, null otherwise.

      • getAttribute

        java.lang.String getAttribute(java.lang.String name)
        Get the value of the attribute given the name of the attribute.Attributes are listed at Attribute List
        Parameters:
        name - - name of the attribute value being sought.
        Returns:
        the attribute value if it exists, null otherwise.

      • toHTML

        java.lang.String toHTML()
        Converts this object into a HTML clickable reference (HREF) in the Output Window of some Capital applications: Capital Logic, Capital Design, Capital Integrator and Capital HarnessXC.

        Will use the name of the object as the message in the link.

        NOTE: some objects do not support 'HREF' links i.e. IXDesign & IXProject objects. In this case, the name of the object will be returned with no HTML tags.

        Returns:
        the HREF link test1

      • toHTML

        java.lang.String toHTML(java.lang.String message)
        Converts this object into a HTML clickable reference (HREF) in the Output Window of some Capital applications: Capital Logic, Capital Design, Capital Integrator and Capital HarnessXC.

        Will use the given 'message' as the message for the 'HREF'.

        NOTE: some objects do not support 'HREF' links i.e. IXDesign & IXProject objects. In this case, the 'message' parameter will be returned with no HTML tags.

        Parameters:
        message - - the message to use.
        Returns:
        the HREF link

      • getID

        java.lang.String getID()
        Get the ID of the object
        Returns:
        the ID of the object

      • getBaseID

        java.lang.String getBaseID()
        Get the Base ID of the object
        Returns:
        the Base ID of the object

      • getParentID

        java.lang.String getParentID()
        Get the Parent ID of the object
        Returns:
        the Parent ID of the object

      • getSymbol

        IXSymbol getSymbol()
        Get the referenced symbol object, if this is an object with symbol. Note: Even if in diagram there is a symbol it doesn't necessarily mean that symbol is directly associated to the connectivity objecct and in that case this API return null. For example connector in Capital Logic will have direct symbol association but for the connector in Capital HarnessXC there can be multiple symbol representation in different diagrams and is driven from style set associated to the diagram.
        Returns:
        the referenced symbol of the object if it exists, null otherwise
